description: Output is duplicated after a restart because the sink is neither idempotent nor transactional.
body: Failure mode: a job advertised as exactly-once duplicates writes after a checkpoint recovery because, while engine state recovers correctly, the sink re-applies records that were emitted before the failure but not yet acknowledged. Detection signals: downstream totals overcount after a restart, and a recovery drill shows sink output exceeding input. Do not treat the duplicates as a transient glitch. Safer fallback: make the sink idempotent via upserts keyed on a stable event id, or use a transactional two-phase-commit sink, then re-run the recovery drill and confirm output counts match input exactly before claiming exactly-once.
